target.title: docs(channels): document ackReactionScope for Slack & Telegram (DM gotcha)
target.body:
## Summary

The Slack and Telegram channel docs cover `ackReaction` (which emoji to send) but don't mention `ackReactionScope` (when to actually send it). Because the default scope is `group-mentions`, setting `ackReaction: "eyes"` on a Slack DM-only deployment looks broken: the bot has the `reactions:write` scope, the config validates, but no reaction ever fires. The actual reason — the scope gate explicitly excludes DMs — only shows up in `src/channels/ack-reactions.ts` and `docs/channels/matrix.md`.

This PR adds that gotcha to the Slack and Telegram channel docs.

## Changes

- **`docs/channels/slack.md`**
  - Split *Ack reactions* into "Emoji (`ackReaction`)" and "Scope (`messages.ackReactionScope`)".
  - Document the full scope enum (`all`, `direct`, `group-all`, `group-mentions`, `off`/`none`).
  - Add a `<Note>` callout flagging that the default does **not** fire in DMs, and that `messages.ackReactionScope` is read at Slack provider startup (gateway restart required).
  - Add a short JSON example using `ackReactionScope: "all"`.
- **`docs/channels/telegram.md`** — same structure inside the existing `<Accordion>`.

Slack and Telegram runtime today reads scope from `messages.ackReactionScope` only (no per-account/per-channel override) — that's what the second commit clarifies after reviewer feedback. No behavior changes; docs only.

## Real behavior proof

Reproduced the surprise locally before writing, then applied the docs' recommendation and confirmed it works.

**Before** (`messages.ackReactionScope` unset, defaulting to `"group-mentions"`):
- Slack DM to the bot → no `:eyes:` reaction, even though `channels.slack.ackReaction: "eyes"` is configured and the bot token has `reactions:write`.

**Config change applied:**
```json
{ "messages": { "ackReactionScope": "all" } }
```

**Gateway log shows the config change being detected:**
```
May 19 16:48:10 dr-claw node[25218]: 2026-05-19T16:48:10.384+00:00 [reload] config change detected; evaluating reload (messages.ackReactionScope)
```

(The reload was not hot-reload-eligible, which is why the docs note a gateway restart is required.)

**After** (gateway restarted with `ackReactionScope: "all"`):

`conversations.history` for the DM channel, filtered to inbound messages (sender `U09L0LJ8W75`), shows the bot now adds `:eyes:` to inbound messages:

```
1779211222.646139  Check on the status of that PR and see what the reviews have  :eyes:x1 :hammer_and_wrench:x1 :hourglass_flowing_sand:x1
1779211032.873419  I think that is a good idea, but it is your memory to use, s  :eyes:x1
```

(`:hammer_and_wrench:` and `:hourglass_flowing_sand:` are normal tool-progress and queued-work indicators; the relevant signal is the `:eyes:` reaction now appearing on inbound DM messages.)

Same Slack workspace before the change: zero `:eyes:` reactions on any inbound DM messages over the prior 40+ inbound messages. Same workspace, same bot, same emoji shortcode — the only thing that changed was `messages.ackReactionScope`.

## Test plan

- `pnpm exec node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/slack.md docs/channels/telegram.md` → passes.
- `pnpm exec node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check` → clean (full docs corpus).
- `pnpm dlx markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/slack.md docs/channels/telegram.md` → 0 errors.

## AI-assisted disclosure

Drafted and verified by an automated assistant operating on the user's behalf (AI-assisted). Human-run repro was performed end-to-end in the live OpenClaw deployment shown above before the docs were written.
target.author: drclaw-iq
